title: User-controlled content is sent to Discord without sanitizing mentions or Markdown

evidence.quote:
const lines = [
      `## 🛟 Support Request`,
      `**Issue:** ${issueType}`,
      description ? `**Description:** ${description}` : null,
      ``,
      `### Metadata`,
      `- **Wallet:** ${wallet ?? "not connected"}`,
      `- **Chain ID:** ${chainId ?? "unknown"}`,
      `- **Connection:** ${connectionStatus}`,
      `- **Route:** ${route}`,
      escrowId ? `- **Escrow ID:** \`${escrowId}\`` : null,
      contractState != null ? `- **Contract State:** ${contractState}` : null,
      lastTxHash ? `- **Last Tx:** \`${lastTxHash}\`` : null,
      `- **Browser:** ${browser}`,
      `- **Time:** ${timestamp}`,
    ].filter(Boolean).join("\n");

reasoning: Fields from the request body are interpolated directly into the Discord message. A submitter can include Discord mention syntax such as @everyone, role/user mentions, or crafted Markdown/backticks in description, issueType, route, browser, and other fields. That allows nuisance notifications and misleading rendered messages in the support channel.
reproduction: POST {"issueType":"@everyone","description":"```fake block```","browser":"@here"} to /api/support and inspect the rendered Discord webhook content.
recommendation: Escape or neutralize Discord mentions and Markdown-sensitive characters for all user-controlled fields, or use Discord webhook allowed_mentions with an empty parse list if supported by the webhook payload.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests validate rendering or sanitization of Discord-bound user input.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that submits @everyone/@here and Markdown delimiters, then asserts the webhook payload cannot trigger mentions and renders the content as inert text.
